questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a page table in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation
B) To translate virtual addresses to physical addresses
C) To handle interrupts
D) To manage process synchronization
questions.explanation: A page table is used to translate virtual addresses into physical addresses, which is essential for memory management.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a page table?
options: A) It is a fixed-size data structure
B) It is a dynamic data structure
C) It is used for memory protection
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A page table is a dynamic data structure that is used for memory protection and mapping virtual pages to physical pages.

questions.question: What happens when a process requests a page that is not in physical memory?
options: A) The operating system allocates more memory
B) The operating system swaps out a page from memory
C) The operating system blocks the process
D) The operating system creates a new page
questions.explanation: When a process requests a page that is not in physical memory, the operating system swaps out a page from memory to free up space for the requested page.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of page table entry?
options: A) Page number
B) Frame number
C) Both A and B
D) Neither A nor B
questions.explanation: A page table entry can include both the page number and the frame number, which are used to map virtual pages to physical pages.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using a page table over a segment table?
options: A) It is more efficient in terms of memory usage
B) It is more efficient in terms of access time
C) It provides better memory protection
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A page table provides better memory protection than a segment table because it can be used to implement mandatory access control and memory protection mechanisms.
